2605.05818.title: LeakDojo: Decoding the Leakage Threats of RAG Systems
2605.05818.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enables large language models (LLMs) to leverage external knowledge, but also exposes valuable RAG databases to leakage attacks. As RAG systems grow more complex and LLMs exhibit stronger instruction-following capabilities, existing studies fall short of systematically assessing RAG leakage risks. We present LeakDojo, a configurable framework for controlled evaluation of RAG leakage. Using LeakDojo, we benchmark six existing attacks across fourteen LLMs, four datasets, and diverse RAG systems. Our study reveals that (1) query generation and adversarial instructions contribute independently to leakage, with overall leakage well approximated by their product; (2) stronger instruction-following capability correlates with higher leakage risk; and (3) improvements in RAG faithfulness can introduce increased leakage risk. These findings provide actionable insights for understanding and mitigating RAG leakage in practice. Our codebase is available at https://github.com/yeasen-z/LeakDojo.
2605.05818.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）使大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠利用外部知識，但也使得有價值的RAG數據庫面臨洩漏攻擊的風險。隨著RAG系統變得越來越複雜，LLMs展現出更強的指令遵循能力，現有研究未能系統性地評估RAG洩漏風險。我們提出了LeakDojo，一個可配置的框架，用於控制評估RAG洩漏。使用LeakDojo，我們在十四個LLMs、四個數據集和多樣的RAG系統中基準測試了六種現有攻擊。我們的研究揭示了：（1）查詢生成和對抗性指令獨立地對洩漏做出貢獻，整體洩漏可通過它們的乘積良好近似；（2）更強的指令遵循能力與更高的洩漏風險相關；以及（3）RAG忠實度的改善可能引入更高的洩漏風險。這些發現為理解和減輕實際中的RAG洩漏提供了可行的見解。我們的代碼庫可在 https://github.com/yeasen-z/LeakDojo 獲得。
2605.05818.author: Maosen Zhang et.al.
2605.05818.authors: Maosen Zhang, Jianshuo Dong, Boting Lu, Wenyue Li, Xiaoping Zhang, Tianwei Zhang, Han Qiu
